task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window using a command like 'cat' or a text editor showing the contents of ~/Desktop/uuv_spec.txt.

task_completion.description: Build the axisymmetric hull in OpenVSP
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenVSP geometry window showing a cylindrical or tapered hull (fuselage) component. The parameter window showing Length = 3.5 and Max Diameter = 0.35 is strong evidence.

task_completion.description: Add cruciform tail fins to the model
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenVSP geometry window showing 4 symmetric tail fins (cruciform configuration) attached near the aft section of the hull.

task_completion.description: Add a 3-blade aft propeller to the model
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenVSP geometry window showing a propeller component with exactly 3 blades positioned at the very rear of the vehicle (X = 3.50).
OpenVSP CompGeom analysis dialog or results window open, displaying calculated values for Theoretical Volume and Wetted Area.
OpenVSP save dialog, file manager, or terminal confirming 'uuv_model.vsp3' is saved in the ~/Documents/OpenVSP/ directory.
Text editor showing 'uuv_hydro_report.txt' saved on the Desktop, containing the Theoretical Volume and Wetted Area values obtained from the CompGeom analysis.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenVSP GUI to build the model and run the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the OpenVSP graphical interface (adding components, changing parameters, opening the CompGeom tool) rather than generating the .vsp3 file purely via terminal scripts.

integrity.description: Report values match the actual CompGeom output
integrity.visual_evidence: The Theoretical Volume and Wetted Area values typed into the final text report must exactly match the numbers displayed in the OpenVSP CompGeom results window.

integrity.description: Agent did not skip the modeling process to write the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must build the model and run the CompGeom analysis before writing the final report. The report should not be created at the very beginning of the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The UUV model must have a hull length of 3.50 m, max diameter of 0.35 m, and a 3-blade propeller with a 0.25 m diameter. A correctly built model will yield a Theoretical Volume between 0.20 and 0.32 m³, and a Wetted Area between 2.5 and 4.0 m² in the CompGeom analysis. The final report must be saved to ~/Desktop/uuv_hydro_report.txt and the model to ~/Documents/OpenVSP/uuv_model.vsp3.
